deep_dive.title: Scalable and Trustworthy Earth Observation Foundation Models
deep_dive.abstract: Foundation models (FMs) have transformed machine learning from isolated task-specific model development toward general-purpose models pretrained on broad data and adapted to multiple downstream tasks. Earth observation (EO) is an important domain for this paradigm because satellite and airborne archives are large, high-revisit, and increasingly multimodal, while reliable field labels are often sparse. Remote sensing foundation models (RSFMs) cannot be transferred reliably/optimally without domain-specific adaptation. This is because EO data are governed by measurement physics and operational decision constraints. This chapter reviews the design principles arising from these domain-specific constraints. It first defines the FMs paradigm in remote sensing (RS), then synthesizes the current model landscape, pretraining objectives, architecture designs, downstream adaptation and trustworthiness requirements. The chapter also incorporates recent benchmark evidence showing that no single geospatial foundation model is universally best and that inconsistent evaluation remains a major issue to fair comparison and reliable deployment. In addition, two brief environmental monitoring case studies; physics-informed spectral targeted masking for harmful algal bloom prediction and reinforcement learning for adaptive environmental monitoring station selection to illustrate the FMs domain-guided principles in practice. This chapter posits that next-generation RSFMs should be evaluated not only by benchmark accuracy, but also by modality-aware transfer and physically plausible representations for trustworthy EO decisions.

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Reviews design principles for scalable and trustworthy Earth observation foundation models, covering RSFM adaptation challenges, measurement physics constraints, and operational decision constraints.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该综述论文系统回顾了地球观测基础模型的设计原则，指出遥感基础模型（RSFMs）不能直接迁移，需领域特定适应。论文讨论了测量物理和运行决策约束对模型设计的影响，并展望了可扩展和可信赖的EO基础模型发展方向。为理解遥感基础模型现状与挑战提供了全面视角。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 我想查找有关遥感基础模型的论文, especially work that connects or combines: What are foundation models for remote sensing?; Pretrained models for remote sensing tasks; Foundation models in earth observation; Vision transformers for remote sensing; Geospatial foundation models; papers on foundation models for remote sensing image analysis; large scale pretrained models for remote sensing applications.

deep_dive.title: ZipDepth: Bringing Lightweight Zero-Shot Monocular Depth Anywhere, on Any Device
deep_dive.abstract: Monocular depth estimation has seen remarkable progress through foundation models achieving robust zero-shot generalization, yet their computational demands place them far beyond the reach of embedded and mobile platforms. Lightweight alternatives exist, but have been developed almost exclusively within single-domain, self-supervised paradigms, failing silently under domain shift. We present ZipDepth, a compact monocular depth network that bridges this gap by combining an efficient reparameterizable encoder-decoder with large-scale knowledge distillation from a foundation model over a large multi-domain training set. Comprising just 6.1M parameters, ZipDepth runs at real-time rates from server GPUs to power-constrained devices, achieving the best trade-off between zero-shot accuracy and deployment efficiency among lightweight models across five benchmarks, taking a significant step towards the accuracy of foundation models with 50x more parameters.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Addresses domain shift for zero-shot depth estimation via foundation model distillation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过基础模型蒸馏解决零样本深度估计中的域偏移问题
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过基础模型蒸馏解决零样本深度估计中的域偏移问题
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过基础模型蒸馏解决零样本深度估计中的域偏移问题
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ZipDepth combines efficient reparameterizable encoder-decoder with knowledge distillation from a foundation model to achieve zero-shot depth estimation robust to domain shift, with only 6.1M parameters.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对单目深度估计基础模型计算量大且轻量模型无法应对域偏移的问题，本文提出ZipDepth，通过高效的重新参数化编码器-解码器结构和从基础模型的大规模知识蒸馏，在仅6.1M参数下实现实时零样本深度估计，并在多域数据上训练以增强泛化能力。

deep_dive.title: Latent Clarity: Bridging World-Model Kinematics to Semantic Manifolds for Video Anomaly Anticipation
deep_dive.abstract: Continuous video anomaly detection is dominated by reactive Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) that collapses spatiotemporal features into scalar scores. We introduce PULS (Predictive Unified Latent Space), a continuous semantic world-model pipeline comprising two modules: a 490M-parameter KSD Bridge (Kinematic-to-Semantic Distillation) and a 16.8M-parameter Anticipatory State Predictor (ASP). The KSD Bridge maps V-JEPA 2 physical tensors into the 2048-d Qwen3-VL-Embedding-2B text-aligned hypersphere, trained on a subset of UCF-Crime. This translation alone yields a chunk-level AUROC of 0.8994 for UCF-Crime and 0.8162 for out-of-distribution XD-Violence without MIL or hierarchical fusion.   We introduce and validate the Latent Clarity Hypothesis: because JEPA's temporal predictor discards aleatoric pixel noise while preserving kinematics, anticipated future representations are more semantically separable than observed presents. The ASP sharpens these anticipated future latents, achieving 44.5% mean 14-way zero-shot VQA accuracy (exceeding observation baseline by +9.6 pp). Applying the ASP to Observation Tensors collapses accuracy to 7.3% (random chance), proving Anticipation and Observation occupy distinct sub-manifolds. A Triple-Track Lead-Time protocol with an L1-surprise gate yields a peak +8.9 pp anticipatory advantage at T-0.5s (p < 0.001, N = 1,000 permutation), separating physical anticipation from static scene priors. Zero-shot transfer to XD-Violence confirms that Newtonian-invariant kinematic representations generalize out-of-distribution.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: World-model pipeline for video anomaly anticipation using V-JEPA and Qwen3-VL

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: PULS introduces a world-model pipeline combining kinematic-to-semantic distillation and anticipatory state prediction for video anomaly detection, achieving high AUROC on UCF-Crime and XD-Violence.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视频异常检测中被动多实例学习忽略时空语义的问题，提出PULS持续语义世界模型管线。包含490M参数的KSD桥，将V-JEPA物理张量映射到Qwen3-VL文本对齐超球面，以及16.8M参数的预测状态预测器。在UCF-Crime上达到0.8994的片段级AUROC，在分布外XD-Violence上为0.8162，无需MIL或层级融合。

quick_skim.title: STRATOS: Bridging the Symbolic-to-Numeric Gap in Spatio-Temporal Text-to-SQL for Meteorological Data
quick_skim.abstract: Copernicus, the European Union's Earth observation program, produces petabytes of Earth observation and climate data, offering immense potential for research, policy, and applications. However, access to these datasets requires advanced programming skills and familiarity with domain-specific formats such as NetCDF or GRIB. Moreover, general-purpose Text-to-SQL systems fail when applied naively to the meteorological domain due to a profound ``Symbolic-to-Numeric'' gap. To overcome these limitations, we present an end-to-end Text-to-SQL framework specifically engineered for real-world, scalable meteorological data exploration. Our system intercepts natural language to resolve spatial and semantic ambiguities \textit{before} SQL generation. We design STRATOS, a Spatio-Temporal Resolution Agent for Text-to-SQL to dynamically bridge the symbolic-to-numeric gap by mapping fuzzy concepts to a localized ontology and resolving spatial entities via external knowledge bases. Further, our complexity-aware query rewriter rewrites expensive spatial predicates, reducing execution times from hours to seconds. Last, we introduce the STRATOS Evaluation Workload, comprising 7,520 complex query pairs explicitly designed by domain experts to test scalability and symbolic-to-numeric translation across challenging spatio-temporal dimensions previously unexplored by Text-to-SQL systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 气象数据的时空文本到SQL
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 气象数据的时空文本到SQL
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 气象数据的时空文本到SQL
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a Text-to-SQL framework specifically for meteorological data that resolves spatial and semantic ambiguities before SQL generation, enabling non-experts to query Earth observation datasets.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 气象和地球观测数据访问要求专业编程技能，阻碍了科学应用。提出STRATOS框架，在生成SQL前自然解析自然语言中的空间和语义歧义，弥合符号到数值鸿沟，在真实气象数据上显著提升查询准确率，使非专家能通过自然语言探索NetCDF等专业数据，降低了数据使用门槛，为大规模地球观测数据分析提供便捷接口。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 气象和地球观测数据访问要求专业编程技能，阻碍了科学应用。提出STRATOS框架，在生成SQL前自然解析自然语言中的空间和语义歧义，弥合符号到数值鸿沟，在真实气象数据上显著提升查询准确率，使非专家能通过自然语言探索NetCDF等专业数据，降低了数据使用门槛，为大规模地球观测数据分析提供便捷接口。
